title: Laser Security System
project_description: Laser Security System is an electronic security device that uses a laser beam and an LDR (Light Dependent Resistor) to detect any interruption in the laser path. When the laser beam is interrupted by any object, the LDR senses the change in light intensity and sends the signal to Arduino Uno. Then Arduino activates a buzzer and LED as an alert indication. This system can be used for home security, object detection and intrusion alerts.
objective: To design a simple and effective laser-based security system.
To detect any interruption in the laser beam path.
To trigger an alarm (buzzer) and visual indication (LED) when intrusion is detected.
To provide a low-cost and reliable security solution.

circuit_connections.Common: All GND connections are connected together

circuit_diagram_description: Laser module is placed facing the LDR sensor. LDR is connected to analog pin A0 using a 10K resistor as a voltage divider. LED is connected to digital pin 8 and buzzer is connected to digital pin 9 of Arduino Uno. All components share a common ground.
working_principle: Laser beam continuously falls on the LDR sensor.
When the beam is interrupted, the resistance of the LDR changes.
Arduino detects the change in analog value.
Arduino turns ON the buzzer and LED to indicate an intrusion.
conclusion: The Laser Security System successfully detects any interruption in the laser beam and provides an alert through buzzer and LED. It is simple, cost-effective and useful for home and office security applications. This project helps in understanding Arduino interfacing, LDR sensors and basic security systems.
desc: Laser Security System using Arduino, LDR, and a laser module to detect intrusions.

title: Automatic School Bell and Announcement System
desc: Learn to build a Automatic School Bell and Announcement System.
fullDesc: In this project, we will build a Automatic School Bell and Announcement System. This project will introduce you to the necessary components, coding logic, and assembly required.
author: Tinkering Lab

title: Arduino Flight Simulator
subtitle: Dual joystick flight controller with Arduino + Web Simulator
desc: Build a custom flight controller using dual joysticks and fly in a web simulator!
fullDesc: Build a real flight simulator controller using two joystick modules and an Arduino UNO. The controller reads analog input from both joysticks and streams data over Serial to a flight simulator application running on your PC.
author: Megha Kadam
innovation: A low-cost, student-built flight controller using off-the-shelf components and a 3D printed enclosure, bridging hardware and simulation software.
problemStatement: Commercial flight simulator controllers are expensive and inaccessible for student learning environments.
solutionApproach: Used dual joystick modules with an Arduino UNO to capture analog flight inputs and stream them via Serial to a web-based flight simulator. Housed in a custom 3D printed controller box for a professional feel.

sensors: Dual Axis Joystick Module x2 (Analog X/Y output)

guide: Connect Joystick 1 VCC to Arduino 5V, GND to GND, VRX to A0, VRY to A1
Connect Joystick 2 VCC to Arduino 5V, GND to GND, VRX to A2, VRY to A3
Place both joystick modules inside the 3D printed controller box
Connect the Arduino to your laptop via USB cable
Open Arduino IDE and paste the provided sketch
Upload the sketch to the Arduino UNO
Open Serial Monitor at 9600 baud to verify joystick values are streaming
Launch the Flight Simulator or Web Simulator and configure it to read Serial input
Test joystick movements and verify corresponding simulator response

title: Smart Vertical Axis Wind Turbine
subtitle: Kinetic harvesting with automatic dark-activated indicator
desc: Build an omnidirectional wind turbine that generates power and senses ambient light!
fullDesc: Build a functional Vertical Axis Wind Turbine (VAWT) using a Savonius rotor to drive a DC dynamo motor generator, paired with a BC547 transistor circuit that automatically triggers an indicator when night falls...
author: Siddharth
innovation: An omnidirectional wind harvester integrated with an automatic light-sensing emergency load circuit for school yard applications.
problemStatement: Standard clean energy setups require manual switching or expensive digital controllers to manage stored energy or trigger night indicators.
solutionApproach: Utilized a high-efficiency vertical drag rotor coupled with a DC dynamo motor to harvest wind energy into a DC battery bank. Integrated an analog BC547 NPN transistor switching circuit with an LDR to automatically engage low-power indicators based on ambient light conditions.

hardwareSpecs.powerConsumption: Idle: ~0.5mA, Active Generation: Variable based on wind speed

guide: Assemble the vertical rotor blades onto the central shaft and couple it directly to the axle of the DC dynamo motor.
Connect the positive output of the dynamo motor through a protection diode to the positive terminal of the DC Battery to store generated power safely.
Place the BC547 NPN transistor on the breadboard (Pins: Emitter, Base, Collector).
Connect the 100kΩ resistor from the positive battery rail to the Base pin of the BC547 transistor.
Connect the LDR sensor between the Base pin of the BC547 transistor and the Ground/Negative battery rail to form a voltage divider.
Connect an indicator LED (with appropriate current resistor) or secondary load from the Positive rail to the Collector pin of the transistor.
Connect the Emitter pin of the BC547 directly to the Ground/Negative rail.
Spin the wind turbine to confirm battery charging, and cover the LDR sensor to test the automatic dark-activated switching mechanism.

youtubeVideos.title: Smart VAWT Project Demo
youtubeVideos.desc: Demonstrating wind power generation and automatic dark-switching using the BC547

title: Smart Accident Detection & Alert System
subtitle: ESP8266 + MPU6050 based real-time accident detection
desc: Detect vehicle accidents using an IMU and instantly send emergency alerts over Wi-Fi.
fullDesc: This project uses an ESP8266 NodeMCU and an MPU6050 IMU sensor to monitor vehicle motion in real time. The system continuously analyzes acceleration and gyroscope data to detect severe impacts, sudden orientation changes, and post-impact inactivity. Once an accident is confirmed, it activates a warning countdown allowing the driver to cancel false alarms. If no cancellation is made, the ESP8266 connects to Wi-Fi and sends an emergency notification to predefined contacts through an online service such as Telegram, Firebase, or a custom web server. The system provides a low-cost IoT-based accident detection solution for improving emergency response.
author: Nikhil
innovation: A low-cost IoT-based accident detection system capable of identifying vehicle crashes using only an ESP8266 and MPU6050, without requiring expensive automotive hardware.
problemStatement: Road accidents often delay emergency response because nearby people or family members are not immediately informed. Existing vehicle safety systems are expensive and unavailable in many two-wheelers and budget vehicles.
solutionApproach: The ESP8266 continuously reads acceleration and gyroscope values from the MPU6050. The firmware detects high-impact acceleration, sudden orientation changes, and a period of inactivity after impact. When all conditions are satisfied, a 15-second countdown begins. If the cancel button is not pressed, the ESP8266 connects to Wi-Fi and sends an emergency alert through Telegram, Firebase, or a web server.
guide: Connect MPU6050 SDA to D2 (GPIO4) and SCL to D1 (GPIO5).
Connect MPU6050 VCC to 3.3V and GND to GND.
Connect an  buzzer to D8.
Connect an cancel push button to D7 using INPUT_PULLUP.
Install Wire and MPU6050 libraries in Arduino IDE.
Enter your Wi-Fi SSID and password in the code.
Upload the sketch to ESP8266.
Open Serial Monitor at 115200 baud.
Verify accelerometer and gyroscope readings.
Test with normal movement and simulated impacts.
Confirm that emergency alerts are sent only during actual accident conditions.

youtubeVideos.title: Smart Accident Detection System Demo
youtubeVideos.desc: Demonstration of accident detection and emergency alert notification.

achievements: Prototype for Smart Vehicle Safety System

title: Sketching Live
desc: Sketch on your browser and display it live on a physical TFT screen over USB!
fullDesc: Draw on a digital canvas and watch your art render live on a physical TFT LCD display! Paint Connect Studio Pro connects to your hardware over Web Serial, using custom Run-Length Encoding (RLE) to stream paint commands directly to an ESP32 or Arduino. Choose from various tools, shapes, colors, and line thicknesses.
components: Web Browser with Web Serial API support
author: Amogha
guide: Connect your microcontroller to the computer via USB
Open the Sketching Live app and click CONNECT
Select your device's COM port
Use tools, colors, and shapes to sketch on the canvas
Watch your artwork sync live to the hardware display!
